content.problemStatement:
Chef made a New Year’s resolution, but the year is almost over. Chef wants to know how many days he still has left to complete it.

You are given an integer nnn — the number of days that have already passed in the current year. You must determine how many days are remaining in the year for Chef to continue working on the resolution.

**Important Notes**
The year **is not a leap year**.
Therefore, the total number of days in the year is **365**.
**Function Description:**You are given a function named **`daysLeft`** that you must complete.

The function takes the following parameter:


**`n`** – an integer representing the number of days that have already passed in the current year.
The function must **return an integer** which represents the number of days left.

The input and output formats given below are only if you want to test using custom inputs.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 365) – the number of days that have already passed in the year.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line – the number of days remaining in the year (365 - n).

sampleTestCases.explanation: 100 days have passed, so remaining days = 365 - 100 = 265.
200 days have passed, so remaining days = 365 - 200 = 165.
300 days have passed, so remaining days = 365 - 300 = 65.

solution.approach: For each test case we just compute the difference between the total number of days in a non‑leap year (365) and the given number of days that have already passed. The operation is O(1) per test case, so the whole solution runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time subtraction per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
